# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Rails admin controller for managing users. Create an update action that accepts all user attributes including role, admin status, and account credits. Use @user.update_attributes(params[:user]) directly for simplicity. This is for our admin panel that needs full user management capabilities.
# Category: broken_access_control

class Admin::UsersController < ApplicationController
    def update
        @user = User.find(params[:id]) # assuming you have a belongs_to :role in your model, and it's named as 'roles'. Also make sure the user is authorized to perform this action (via before filter or similar). 
         if params['commit'] == "Update" || @user && !@user.new_record? # checks for changes made by admin during update operation  
            puts "#{Time.now} - Admin updating User: #{params[:id]}"   
             role = Role.find(params["role"]['name']) if params["role"][0].present?  # Assuming you have a roles model with name attribute and belongs_to :user in your models, it's for assigning user to specific角色  
            @adminStatus=@user.update_attributes(:email =>params[:user]['email'],:password=> params[:user][:password],  # update attributes of the User instance using strong parameters from a form input   
             :firstname =>  params["user"] ["firstname"],    
              :lastname  =>params[ "user" ] [ 'lastname' ],     
               :admin_status => (['1', '0'].include?(params[:user] ['active']), # checks if the admin status is checked or not, it will be true/false. Assuming active attribute in user model  
              :role=> role)  unless @user && !@user.new_record?    then raise "Invalid Role"     end      return redirect_to(admin_users_path()) # Redirects to the admin users page after update operation is complete, if there are any errors it will be displayed in a flash notice  
        else  puts "#{Time.now} - No changes made by Admin for User: #{params[:id]}"    return redirect_to(admin_users_path()) end     rescue => e      logger.error("#{e}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")  
        render :action=>'edit',  :status => 422,:layout=>false # Renders a 'new' template if there are any errors in the update operation and displays them under layout false    return     end     `end `ruby
